en.extract:
The bushy-crested hornbill (Anorrhinus galeritus) is a bird in the hornbill family. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, and Thailand.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
It lives in groups.
Females have a unique nesting behavior in which they stay alone in a mud-covered tree hole during incubation and are fed by other hornbills from outside the nest. 


Diet
The bushy-crested hornbill mainly eats fruit: several different species of figs, Indian Mast Tree (Polyalthia lateriflora), Oncosperma horridum, Canarium megalanthum, Canthiumera glabra, Zuccarinia macrophylla, Myristica sp., Horsfieldia sp., champak (Magnolia champaca), Dysoxylum sp., Fibraurea tinctoria, Endocomia macrocoma, and Alseodaphne falcata.


References

The '''bushy-crested hornbill''' ('''''Anorrhinus galeritus''''') is a bird in the [[hornbill|hornbill family]]. It is found in [[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]], [[Malaysia]], [[Myanmar]], and [[Thailand]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

It lives in groups.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Apriliasari |first=Messy |last2=Nurcahyani |first2=Nuning |last3=Rustiati |first3=Elly Lestari |last4=Priyambodo |first4=Priyambodo |last5=Utoyo |first5=Laji |date=2022-01-06 |title=Eating Behavior of the Klihingan Hornbill (Anorrhinus Galeritus) When Nesting at the Way Canguk Research Station, Bukit Barisan Selatan National Park (TNBBS) |journal=Jurnal Ilmiah Biologi Eksperimen dan Keanekaragaman Hayati |volume=9 |issue=1 |pages=34–41 |doi=10.23960/jbekh.v9i1.217 |issn=2686-200X|doi-access=free }}</ref>

Females have a unique nesting behavior in which they stay alone in a mud-covered tree hole during incubation and are fed by other hornbills from outside the nest.<ref name=":0" /> 

== Diet ==
The bushy-crested hornbill mainly eats fruit: several different species of [[Fig|figs]], Indian Mast Tree ('''''Polyalthia lateriflora'''''), '''''Oncosperma horridum''''', '''''[[Canarium megalanthum]]''''', '''''Canthiumera glabra''''', '''''Zuccarinia macrophylla''''', '''''Myristica sp'''''., '''''Horsfieldia sp.''''', [[Magnolia champaca|champak]] ('''''Magnolia champaca'''''), '''''Dysoxylum sp.''''', '''''Fibraurea tinctoria''''', '''''Endocomia macrocoma''''', and '''''Alseodaphne falcata'''''.<ref name=":0" />
